talk_metadata.session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
rhetorical_signals: I'm a little bit nervous because I haven't presented this before
This is still a work in progress
maybe we need actually better professional antigen-presenting cells to be present at the time of priming to really launch that CD8 positive response

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Tumor intrinsic transcriptional programs; specifically, a shift away from the mesenchymal (MES) phenotype as a mode of acquired resistance to ICB monotherapy.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: we also saw a shift away from the mes transcriptional programs as a mode of acquired resistance to ICB monotherapy
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: a small percentage that actually have longer overall survival

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: T cell exhaustion; speaker noted 'a lot of co-inhibitory receptors present on the tumor-associated T cells'.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: those patients who didn't get dexamethasone were actually able to mount circulating T cell responses
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: dexamethasone is bad. So this is a way to flatline your immune stimulation

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Timing of anti-PD-1 therapy relative to neoantigen vaccination in GBM
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Including MGMT methylated patients who receive Temodar, which generates lymphopenia, potentially confounding the immune response analysis.
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: None explicitly mentioned as missing, but noted that TMB did not make a difference in GBM, unlike other tumors.
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: The speaker is using longitudinal samples to track genomic shifts, reducing this risk.
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample size (close to 40 patients) across four cohorts may be underpowered for definitive timing conclusions.

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on synthetic long peptides and poly-ICLC adjuvant; speaker admits the response is predominantly CD4+ rather than the desired CD8+ response.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: The current approach may be limited by the need for professional APCs to effectively prime CD8+ responses.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker noted that TMB does not portend response in GBM, yet the vaccine strategy is based on targeting somatic mutations (neoantigens) derived from that same mutational burden.

key_data_gaps: No randomized OS data for the GBM cohorts
Limited sample size for single-nuclear sequencing (30-some tumors)

crispro_opportunity.description: Targeting the non-mesenchymal phenotype that emerges as a resistance mechanism to ICB in GBM.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: we could see in the presence of anti-PD-1 therapy that there was actually selection for non-mes genetic lesions over time
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational spine to identify specific genetic lesions driving the non-MES transition to develop targeted interventions.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: search for 'mesenchymal phenotype glioblastoma resistance'

unresolved_questions: Why are the induced responses predominantly CD4+ instead of CD8+?
What is the optimal timing of anti-PD-1 relative to vaccination?

talk_metadata.session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
talk_metadata.talk_title: mRNA Vaccines as Immune Adjuvants to Checkpoint Blockade
rhetorical_signals: we like to propose this very linear story... But in reality, I think that the reality here is a little more complex
this retrospective data we really have to take with a grain of salt
designed not to produce a strong interferon alpha burst

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: The 'linear story' of target selection -> injection -> regression ignores the potent non-specific innate immune activation (interferon alpha) that may be the actual driver of efficacy.
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: GFP RNA nanoparticle vaccine doubles survival in an aggressive metastatic model of osteosarcoma

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Universal mRNA vaccine as an adjuvant to ICI
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Retrospective data suffered from 'healthy vaccinee bias' and 'immortal time bias'.
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Lack of a specific biomarker to predict who will mount the MDA5-mediated interferon alpha response.

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: High cost and long manufacturing lead times (4-8 weeks) make them unsuitable for advanced disease, creating a gap for 'off-the-shelf' universal adjuvants.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: 4-8 weeks for RNA manufacturing; 26 weeks for peptide manufacturing.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Over-reliance on personalization when non-specific innate activation (IFN-alpha) may provide significant benefit.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker notes that COVID vaccines were specifically designed to avoid TLR7 activation (via N1-methylpseudouridine), yet they still trigger a massive interferon alpha surge via the MDA5 'backdoor' pathway.

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective human trial data for the 'universal' adjuvant hypothesis
Mouse model data may not fully translate to human T cell dynamics

crispro_opportunity.description: Developing an off-the-shelf mRNA adjuvant that optimizes the MDA5/IFN-alpha pathway without the need for personalization.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: we have a large ramp of opportunity to dial this type of an effect up and increase the sensitivity to these RNA vaccines
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational modeling to optimize RNA structures that maximize MDA5 activation while minimizing off-target toxicity.

cited_competitors.name: BioNTech
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as the source of the COVID vaccine specifications used in the study.

unresolved_questions: Does a personalized vaccine provide an added benefit over a universal adjuvant in humans?
How to optimize the 'backdoor' MDA5 activation for maximum anti-tumor effect?

talk_metadata.session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
talk_metadata.talk_title: Optimizing Peptide Vaccines and Adoptive Cell Therapy
rhetorical_signals: this is probably motherhood and apple pie
I wish I had some disclosures

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Failure to induce stem-like CD8 T cells (TCF7+/SLAMF6+); instead, they induce short-lived effector cells (Granzyme+/KLRG1+).
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: why weren't those high frequency of CD8 cells by the subcu working?

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Standard expansion for a month leads to effector-like or exhausted cells, reducing durability.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Standard ACT lacks a complementary vaccination strategy to remodel the TME and boost the transferred cells.

key_data_gaps: No human data for the PI3K delta inhibitor expansion protocol
Durability of the stem-like T cell response is unknown (protection wanes after 30 days)

crispro_opportunity.description: Combining PI3K delta inhibitors during T cell expansion with a specific IV vaccine to maintain stemness and remodel the TME.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Stem-like cells are better than effector-like cells, and they need vaccine
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Identify the optimal combination of small molecule inhibitors and vaccine antigens to maximize the TCF1+ stem-like population.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: 'PI3K delta inhibitor T cell stemness'
PubMed: 'TCF7 SLAMF6 CD8 T cells cancer'

cited_competitors.name: Genentech
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned in the context of early peptide response studies (Lelia Delamar).

unresolved_questions: How durable is the protection provided by stem-like T cells?
Do stem-like cells convert quickly to effectors upon tumor entry?

assets_to_track: PI3K delta inhibitor for TIL expansion

talk_metadata.session_title: Cancer Vaccines: The Next Immunotherapy Breakthrough
rhetorical_signals: I think this has not really been exactly known

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Surgical removal of the spleen (splenectomy) removes the critical driver of the immune response for this platform.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: most of the non-responders were asplenic at the time of vaccination

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Clonal evolution; outgrowth of cancer clones that do not harbor the targeted neoantigens.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: resistance appears to occur due to outgrowth of non-vaccine targeted cancer clones

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Conventional thinking focuses on mutation quantity (TMB), but the speaker's 'quality model' shows that high-quality neoantigens in low-TMB tumors (PDAC) are the real drivers of survival.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Current access is limited to lower-risk indications and specific centers; infrastructure for academic-led end-to-end vaccination is missing.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on peripheral blood analysis is insufficient; requires clone-by-clone overlap analysis with tissues to distinguish de novo vs. pre-existing responses.

key_data_gaps: Causation not yet proven (randomized trial ongoing)
Role of CD4 T cells in direct killing vs. helper function is unknown

crispro_opportunity.description: Using a 'neoantigen quality model' (biophysical characteristics of non-selfness) rather than TMB to select patients for vaccination in 'cold' tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: tumors enriched in high quality, but not quantity neoantigens identified patients with longer survival
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate biophysical neoantigen quality scoring into the computational spine to identify targetable antigens in low-TMB cancers.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: check status of Autogene cevumarán randomized trial

cited_competitors.name: BioNTech
cited_competitors.context: Partner in manufacturing the custom vaccines.

cited_competitors.name: GenTech
cited_competitors.context: Partner in manufacturing the custom vaccines.

unresolved_questions: Do vaccine-induced CD4 T cells directly kill tumor cells or only support CD8s?
Does cross-reactivity to the wild-type epitope actually help T cell persistence without causing toxicity?
